title: Document Codex permission policy vs bee gate_bypass + bee-safe/bee-autopilot profiles
action: Per D7 (revised by evidence: approval_policy appears ONLY in this repo's local .codex/config.toml — bee distributes no approval_policy anywhere; verify with grep before writing). Add to INSTALL.md's Codex section and docs/06-runtime-integration.md: (1) Codex's approval/permission policy and bee's gate_bypass are DISTINCT concepts — one governs Codex tool approvals, the other bee's workflow gates; setting one never sets the other; NOTE explicitly that Codex hook TRUST is a third, independent layer neither can override (changed hooks may be skipped pending review). (2) Two recommended profiles: bee-safe (approval_policy on-request; bee gates ask normally) and bee-autopilot (approval_policy never + bee-bypass-gate total) with one-line trade-offs. (3) An explicit statement that bee ships no approval_policy default — the host owner chooses. (4) Truth correction rider (advisor finding 5): after cnr2-7's regeneration the shipped Codex hook event set is EIGHT events including SubagentStart — update the event count/list wherever INSTALL.md and 06-runtime-integration.md state it (cnr2-1 wrote seven). Keep prose voice; do not restructure unrelated sections.

truths: INSTALL.md names both profiles and states bee ships no approval_policy default
the permission-vs-gate_bypass distinction is stated in BOTH files
event count updated to eight/SubagentStart in both files where stated

artifacts.substantive: profiles + distinction + trust-layer prose in the Codex section

prohibitions: No change to any shipped config/template file
No change to this repo's local .codex/config.toml

verify: bash -c 'grep -q "bee-safe" INSTALL.md && grep -q "bee-autopilot" INSTALL.md && grep -qi "gate_bypass" INSTALL.md && grep -qi "gate_bypass" docs/06-runtime-integration.md && grep -qi "SubagentStart" INSTALL.md && ! grep -rn "approval_policy" skills/ && echo PROFILES-DOC-OK'

trace.outcome: Documented Codex approval_policy vs bee gate_bypass distinction, hook-trust as a third independent layer, bee-safe/bee-autopilot profiles, no-shipped-default statement, and the 7->8 event count (SubagentStart) rider in INSTALL.md and docs/06-runtime-integration.md.
trace.verify_command: bash -c 'grep -q "bee-safe" INSTALL.md && grep -q "bee-autopilot" INSTALL.md && grep -qi "gate_bypass" INSTALL.md && grep -qi "gate_bypass" docs/06-runtime-integration.md && grep -qi "SubagentStart" INSTALL.md && ! grep -rn "approval_policy" skills/ && echo PROFILES-DOC-OK'
